Off Calendar Apr-22-2016 Continued To Jul-13-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ADRIAN J. SAWYER, ESQ. of Kerr & Wagstaffe LLP, Tel No. 415-371-8500 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. CANDIE Y. CHANG, ESQ. of Law Offices of Garrett & Tully APC, Tel No. 626-577-9500 appearing for Defendant Linda Montgomery.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT LINDA MONTGOMERY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, Ms. Montgomery's demurrer to the second cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty in the first amended complaint is overruled. Ms. Lange adequately alleges that Ms. Montgomery owed her fiduciary duties based on the allegations that Ms. Lange was Ms. Montgomery's client, Ms. Montgomery had de facto control of Ms. Lange's finances at least insofar as her tax obligations were concerned, and Ms. Montgomery provided financial advice to Ms. Lange. None of the cases relied on by Ms. Montgomery for her demurrer hold that on these or similar facts that there is no fiduciary relationship as a matter of law. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant Linda G. Montgomery, Cpa, To Strike Portions Of First Amended Complaint Of Plaintiff Lynette Lange
Off Calendar Mar-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ADRIAN J. SAWYER, ESQ. of Kerr & Wagstaffe LLP, Tel No. 415-371-8500 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. CANDIE Y. CHANG, ESQ. of Law Offices of Garrett & Tully APC, Tel No. 626-577-9500 appearing for Defendant Linda Montgomery.

Matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Ms. Montgomery's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations is granted with twenty days leave to amend. The first amended complaint does not allege ultimate facts that Ms. Montgomery acted maliciously, fraudulently or oppressively. For instance, the first amended complaint suggests but does not actually allege that Ms. Montgomery issued tax documents to Ms. Lange knowingly that the tax documents were false or for the purpose of requiring Ms. Montgomery to pay more taxes than she was obligated to pay under the relevant tax laws. Instead the first amended complaint appears to allege that Ms. Montgomery negligently issued the tax documents. Plaintiff shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature which may be e-mailed to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
